# Workforce / labour scope template — Carney delivery (I-tpl-008).
#
# Used when the user is researching workforce, labour-market,
# employment, skills, automation displacement, immigration policy
# affecting labour, or productivity questions. The corpus should be
# dominated by labour-statistical agency outputs (T3) plus
# peer-reviewed labour economics research (T1) and policy-institute
# commentary (T6).

domain: workforce
description: >
  Workforce and labour-market corpus for employment trends, skills
  policy, automation displacement, and labour-economics research.
  Statistical-agency outputs and peer-reviewed labour economics are
  the primary evidence; advocacy-group reports require explicit
  funding-disclosure provenance.

inclusion_criteria:
  - "Statistics Canada (StatCan) Labour Force Survey, Survey of Employment Payrolls and Hours, Census long-form."
  - "US Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) — Current Employment Statistics, Current Population Survey, Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey, Productivity and Costs."
  - "OECD Employment Outlook, OECD Skills Outlook, OECD Future of Work."
  - "International Labour Organization (ILO) reports + ILOSTAT."
  - "Eurostat labour-market statistics."
  - "Peer-reviewed labour economics journals — Quarterly Journal of Economics, Journal of Labor Economics, Labour Economics, Industrial Labor Relations Review."
  - "Government-of-Canada Employment and Social Development Canada (ESDC) outputs + provincial labour-market analytics."
  - "Policy-institute reports — Brookings Hamilton Project, Aspen Institute Future of Work, Pew Research Center labour, RBC Climate Institute Future of Work."
  - "World Bank labour-market reports for international comparisons."

exclusion_criteria:
  - "Industry-association studies presented as primary research (allowed only as T6 with funding disclosure)."
  - "Advocacy-group reports without methodology section."
  - "Recruiting / staffing-agency surveys presented as labour-market data (allowed only as T5 vendor tier)."
  - "Op-eds in business media without disclosed analyst affiliation."

expected_tier_distribution:
  - tier: T3
    min_fraction: 0.35
    max_fraction: 0.65
    rationale: >
      Statistical-agency outputs (StatCan, BLS, OECD, ILO, Eurostat)
      are the primary quantitative evidence. Required for any claim
      involving labour-market metrics.
  - tier: T1
    min_fraction: 0.10
    max_fraction: 0.30
    rationale: >
      Peer-reviewed labour economics research papers.
  - tier: T6
    min_fraction: 0.10
    max_fraction: 0.25
    rationale: >
      Policy-institute reports — Brookings Hamilton Project, Aspen,
      Pew, RBC Climate Institute. Analyst tier, NOT peer-reviewed.
  - tier: T2
    min_fraction: 0.05
    max_fraction: 0.20
    rationale: >
      Systematic reviews / meta-analyses of labour-market literature.
  - tier: T5
    min_fraction: 0.00
    max_fraction: 0.10
    rationale: >
      Industry / staffing-agency surveys. Treat with skepticism;
      cite for technical-trade claims only.
  - tier: T4
    min_fraction: 0.00
    max_fraction: 0.10
    rationale: >
      Narrative reviews of workforce trends.

required_jurisdictions:
  - CA
  - US
  - OECD  # cross-country comparisons via OECD aggregates

audit_emphasis:
  - statistical_agency_citation_required_for_quantitative_claims
  - per_jurisdiction_metric_definition_disclosed
  - advocacy_group_funding_disclosure_required

# AI sovereignty scope template — Carney delivery (I-tpl-006).
#
# Used when the user is researching AI sovereignty: data residency,
# compute sovereignty, model provenance, export controls, and the
# regulatory regimes governing AI development across jurisdictions.
# The corpus should be dominated by primary regulatory documents
# (T3), national AI strategy white papers (T3), and credible policy
# commentary (T6). Industry whitepapers are admissible as T5 with
# strong skepticism (frequently marketing-driven).

domain: ai_sovereignty
description: >
  Sovereign-AI corpus emphasizing primary regulatory documents,
  national AI strategies, export-control regimes, and credible
  policy commentary on data + compute + model sovereignty.

inclusion_criteria:
  - "National AI strategies (Canada, EU, UK, US, Japan, Singapore, France, Germany, Israel, India, China where translatable)."
  - "Regulatory documents on AI — EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF, Canada AIDA, US Executive Orders on AI, China interim measures on generative AI, UK AI Safety Institute outputs."
  - "Export-control documents — BIS rules on advanced compute, Wassenaar Arrangement updates, EU dual-use regulation revisions."
  - "Data-residency law — GDPR plus national addenda, PIPEDA, PIPL, LGPD, sectoral health-data laws."
  - "Compute-sovereignty / sovereign-cloud frameworks — Gaia-X, Bharat Cloud, OVH Sovereign, MS / AWS / GCP sovereign-region documentation (treated as T5 — vendor/industry source)."
  - "Peer-reviewed AI-policy journal articles (treated as T1)."
  - "Policy-institute reports from Brookings, RAND, Belfer Center, AI Now Institute, GovAI (treated as T6 — analyst commentary, NOT peer-reviewed)."
  - "Government technology-strategy committee reports."

exclusion_criteria:
  - "Vendor whitepapers presented as primary regulatory interpretation."
  - "Op-eds without disclosed affiliation in general-interest media."
  - "Crypto / Web3 sovereignty claims framed as AI sovereignty (different regulatory regime; cite separately if relevant)."

expected_tier_distribution:
  - tier: T3
    min_fraction: 0.35
    max_fraction: 0.65
    rationale: >
      Regulatory documents (AI Act, executive orders, export controls)
      and national AI strategies are the primary evidence base.
  - tier: T6
    min_fraction: 0.10
    max_fraction: 0.30
    rationale: >
      Policy commentary from named analysts (Brookings, RAND,
      Belfer Center, GovAI). Not peer-reviewed; analyst tier.
  - tier: T2
    min_fraction: 0.05
    max_fraction: 0.20
    rationale: >
      Systematic reviews of AI policy / governance literature.
  - tier: T1
    min_fraction: 0.05
    max_fraction: 0.20
    rationale: >
      Peer-reviewed primary studies on AI governance, data flows,
      and policy outcomes.
  - tier: T5
    min_fraction: 0.00
    max_fraction: 0.15
    rationale: >
      Vendor sovereign-cloud documentation (industry/marketing tier).
      Treat with skepticism; cite for technical claims only, never as
      a primary regulatory interpretation.
  - tier: T4
    min_fraction: 0.00
    max_fraction: 0.10
    rationale: >
      Narrative reviews of the AI sovereignty landscape.

required_jurisdictions:
  - CA  # Canada (priority for Carney)
  - US
  - EU
  - UK

# Per CLAUDE.md §-1.1: per-claim verdicts must cite the EXACT span
# from a regulatory document, not paraphrase. Industry vendor
# claims (T5) flagged with "vendor commentary" badge in the report.
audit_emphasis:

 succeeded in 939ms:
"""M-28 Fix #1 (2026-04-20): authoritative-source query expansion.

Generalizable abstraction: scope templates carry an optional list of
host-shaped strings under `regulatory_anchors`. When present, the
expander emits one extra amplified query per anchor of the form
`{base_question} site:{anchor}`. When absent / empty / malformed,
the expander returns an empty list. The template is the ONLY place
the host list lives; this module has no baked-in host knowledge and
no baked-in query-domain knowledge. It treats anchors as opaque
host-shaped tokens.

Design invariants:
  - Zero host names, zero domain names, zero jurisdictional terms
    anywhere in this module. All such content lives in YAML templates
    and in test fixtures. A repository-level test
    (`test_m28_regulatory_expander_no_hardcoded_hosts`) asserts this
    at CI time so future edits cannot slip in a hard-code by accident.
  - Template-driven: the caller chooses the anchors per domain by
    writing them into the template. Every domain supported by the
    scope_templates/ directory uses the same expander code.
  - Backwards-compatible: missing list / empty list / malformed
    entries all return an empty list, so the expander is safe to call
    unconditionally.

Design:
  - Caller (sweep orchestrator) loads the scope template once and
    calls `expand_regulatory_queries(question, template)` to get the
    extra queries, merges them into the amplified list.
  - The live retriever does not know about the anchor concept; the
    extra queries travel through as regular amplified queries and go
    through the same scope-validator de-drift + Serper/S2 fanout.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import os
from typing import Any

logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.regulatory_expander")


# PG_SWEEP_MAX_REGULATORY_ANCHORS: soft upper bound on how many anchor
# queries this function will emit from a single template. A template
# with more anchors still loads fine; the expander just truncates
# emission to the cap. Set to 0 to disable the cap.
#
# M-43 (2026-04-22): raised default 10 -> 12 after a prior retrieval
# sweep silently truncated the final anchor in an 11-entry template,
# dropping downstream bibliography coverage to zero for that
# jurisdiction. 12 fits the current template with one future-addition
# headroom. Extra retrieval cost per run: ~2 queries, negligible.
_DEFAULT_MAX_ANCHORS = 12


def _max_anchors() -> int:
    """Read the max-anchor cap from env each call (so a test can monkey-
    patch without importing a module-level constant)."""
    raw = os.getenv("PG_SWEEP_MAX_REGULATORY_ANCHORS")
    if raw is None:
        return _DEFAULT_MAX_ANCHORS
    try:
        val = int(raw)
    except (TypeError, ValueError):
        return _DEFAULT_MAX_ANCHORS
    return max(0, val)


def _extract_anchors(template: dict[str, Any] | None) -> list[str]:
    """Pull the `regulatory_anchors` list from a loaded scope template.

    Returns an empty list for missing / malformed / empty configuration.
    Trims whitespace, skips non-string entries and empties.
    """
    if not isinstance(template, dict):
        return []
    raw = template.get("regulatory_anchors")
    if not isinstance(raw, list):
        return []
    anchors: list[str] = []
    for entry in raw:
        if not isinstance(entry, str):
            continue
        stripped = entry.strip().lower()
        # Reject entries that look like full URLs — the `site:` search
        # operator expects a host. A full URL with a path would produce
        # an invalid query. Keep only host-shaped tokens. Also reject
        # entries containing whitespace.
        if not stripped or "/" in stripped or " " in stripped:
            continue
        anchors.append(stripped)
    # Deduplicate while preserving declared order.
    seen: set[str] = set()
    unique: list[str] = []
    for a in anchors:
        if a not in seen:
            seen.add(a)
            unique.append(a)
    return unique


def expand_regulatory_queries(
    question: str,
    template: dict[str, Any] | None,
) -> list[str]:
    """Return `site:{anchor}` queries derived from the scope template.

    Args:
        question: the user's research question (or any base query text).
        template: the loaded scope template dict (or None if none set).

    Returns:
        A list of `{question} site:{anchor}` strings, one per configured
        anchor (up to `PG_SWEEP_MAX_REGULATORY_ANCHORS` entries). Empty
        list when the template has no `regulatory_anchors` key, an
        empty list, or when the question is empty/whitespace.

    This function is pure and deterministic — no network, no state.
    """
    base = (question or "").strip()
    if not base:
        return []
    anchors = _extract_anchors(template)
    if not anchors:
        return []
    cap = _max_anchors()
    if cap > 0 and len(anchors) > cap:
        logger.info(
            "[regulatory_expander] template has %d anchors; capped to %d "
            "via PG_SWEEP_MAX_REGULATORY_ANCHORS",
            len(anchors), cap,
        )
        anchors = anchors[:cap]
    queries = [f"{base} site:{anchor}" for anchor in anchors]
    logger.info(
        "[regulatory_expander] emitted %d anchor queries for base=%r",
        len(queries), base[:60],
    )
    return queries

# Custom / free-form scope template — BUG-B-102 R2b (pipeline-B UI path).
#
# Used when the caller (typically scripts/live_server.py → graph_v4
# UI research endpoint) submits a research question that doesn't fit
# the four core domain templates (clinical, tech, policy, due_diligence).
# The template is deliberately permissive on tier distribution because
# free-form UI queries span topic domains where enforcing a specific
# tier mix would be incorrect.
#
# IMPORTANT: this does NOT disable the hardening invariants. The
# corpus_adequacy_gate, strict_verify, evaluator gate, and delimiter
# sanitization all still fire. It only loosens the tier-distribution
# *expectation* so corpus_approval_gate doesn't auto-reject a diverse
# UI-query corpus.

domain: custom
description: >
  Free-form scope template for UI-submitted research questions that
  don't match clinical / tech / policy / due_diligence. Treats any
  classified source tier as acceptable; the corpus-approval gate
  relies on the operator's substantive note for quality control.

inclusion_criteria:
  - Any source classified at T1-T7 by the tier classifier.
  - Official documentation with clear authorship.
  - Primary-source reporting from reputable outlets.

exclusion_criteria:
  - Unattributed AI-generated summaries.
  - Content-starved URLs (below PG_CRAG_PAYWALL_MIN_CHARS).
  - Known disinformation or retracted sources (per RetractionWatch).

expected_tier_distribution:

  # UI caller may legitimately submit questions where T1 primary
  # research simply doesn't exist (consumer tech, recent events).

    rationale: >
      Trade press and quality journalism.
  - tier: T7
    min_fraction: 0.00
    max_fraction: 0.40
    rationale: >
      Blog posts and opinion — permitted but capped.

# M-28 Fix #1 (2026-04-20): regulatory_anchors are URL hosts that the
# amplification layer should target with dedicated `site:{anchor}`
# queries so the corpus includes authoritative label / monograph /
# guidance content. The list is configurable per domain; empty or
# missing list means no regulatory expansion (backwards-compat).
# Absolutely no hard-coded host lists in Python — only this template.
regulatory_anchors:
  - accessdata.fda.gov   # FDA Drugs@FDA (label, approval history, BLA/NDA docs)
  - ema.europa.eu        # EMA SmPC (Summary of Product Characteristics)
  - fda.gov              # FDA news / safety announcements / guidance
  - hres.ca              # Health Canada Regulatory Decision Summary PDFs
  - pdf.hres.ca          # M-37: Health Canada DPD Product Monograph PDFs
  - canada.ca            # Health Canada Product Monograph search
  - recalls-rappels.canada.ca  # M-37: Canadian safety recalls / DHPP
  - health-products.canada.ca  # M-37: Health Canada Drug Products DB
  - hpfb-dgpsa.ca        # M-42d: HPFB Drug and Health Product Portal (DHPP root)
  - who.int              # WHO Essential Medicines, guidelines
  - nice.org.uk          # UK National Institute for Health and Care Excellence

# M-35 (2026-04-21): primary-trial anchors are trial-name tokens that
# the amplification layer should wrap in quotes and append to the
# question to surface pivotal-trial primary publications (typically
# NEJM / Lancet / JAMA / Nature). Trial names are query-specific (the
# trial program that matters depends on the drug / condition under
# study), so this field is keyed by SWEEP SLUG rather than applied
# globally to the whole clinical domain. Missing slug = no expansion.
# Zero trial names are hard-coded in Python — they live only here.
#
# Covers Codex DR pass-11 gap #1: "Replace the citation mix with
# primary SURPASS-1..SURPASS-6, SURPASS-CVOT, SURMOUNT-2/4 trial
# papers as first-class sources." V23 corpus had 62 trial-mention
# rows but mostly conference abstracts and post-hoc pooled analyses;
# the primary NEJM / Lancet publications for SURPASS-1/2/3 and
# SURMOUNT-1 were absent. Targeted `"SURPASS-1" {question}` queries
# surface the pivotal trial publication directly.
per_query_primary_trial_anchors:
  clinical_tirzepatide_t2dm:
    - SURPASS-1       # Rosenstock et al., Lancet 2021 — monotherapy
    - SURPASS-2       # Frías et al., NEJM 2021 — vs semaglutide
    - SURPASS-3       # Ludvik et al., Lancet 2021 — vs insulin degludec

"""I-carney-003 — public transparency endpoint.

Returns deploy provenance (region, signing key, sovereignty filter coverage,
evaluator models, egress allowlist) so reviewers and Carney's office can
audit that the deploy is sovereign + verifiable.

Three routes:
- GET /transparency         JSON deploy descriptor
- GET /transparency/pubkey.asc  text/plain armored GPG public key
- GET /transparency/policy  JSON full sovereignty + egress policy
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import os
import subprocess
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path

from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException
from fastapi.responses import PlainTextResponse
from pydantic import BaseModel

from polaris_v6 import __version__

router = APIRouter(prefix="/transparency", tags=["transparency"])

POLICY_VERSION = "v1.0"
DEFAULT_PUBKEY_PATH = "/app/gpg/polaris_demo_pubkey.asc"
# Codex diff iter-1 P1-004: /etc/polaris/* only exists on the EC2 host (cloud-
# init copies it there). The api container has /app/config/ baked in by
# Dockerfile.v6 via `COPY config/ config/`. Default to the in-container path
# so /transparency returns the real 17-domain allowlist without operator
# bind-mount; the host /etc/polaris path is used by egress_lockdown.sh only.
DEFAULT_EGRESS_ALLOWLIST = "/app/config/egress_allowlist.txt"


class SovereigntyPolicy(BaseModel):
    """Sovereignty filter description — cited tiers, cascade behavior."""

    cleared_tiers: list[str]
    cascade_rule: str
    tier_definitions: dict[str, str]


class TransparencyResponse(BaseModel):
    """Public deploy descriptor."""

    provider: str
    region: str
    git_commit: str
    polaris_version: str
    deploy_timestamp: str
    signing_key_id: str | None
    signing_key_fingerprint: str | None
    sovereignty_filter: SovereigntyPolicy
    evaluator_models: dict[str, str]
    egress_allowlist: list[str]
    build_time_hosts_pruned: bool
    dependencies: dict[str, list[str]]


def _load_egress_allowlist() -> list[str]:
    """Load the in-container egress allowlist or fall back to 'unrestricted'.

    Default path is /app/config/egress_allowlist.txt (baked into Dockerfile.v6
    via `COPY config/ config/`); operator override via POLARIS_EGRESS_ALLOWLIST.
    """
    path = Path(os.environ.get("POLARIS_EGRESS_ALLOWLIST", DEFAULT_EGRESS_ALLOWLIST))
    if not path.exists():
        return ["unrestricted (no lockdown applied)"]
    return [
        line.strip()
        for line in path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines()
        if line.strip() and not line.strip().startswith("#")
    ]


def _load_dependencies() -> dict[str, list[str]]:
    """Best-effort dependency listing for transparency.

    Reads requirements-v6.txt if present (backend image bakes this in).
    web/package.json may or may not be present in the backend image —
    fail silently and emit empty list rather than 503 on absence.
    """
    deps: dict[str, list[str]] = {"python": [], "node": []}
    py_req = Path("/app/requirements-v6.txt")
    if py_req.exists():
        deps["python"] = [
            line.strip()
            for line in py_req.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines()
            if line.strip() and not line.strip().startswith("#") and not line.strip().startswith("-")
        ][:50]  # cap to keep response small
    # web/package.json optional — backend image doesn't include it.
    return deps


def _git_commit() -> str:
    """Resolve git commit SHA in order:

    1. POLARIS_GIT_COMMIT env var — injected at image build time (Dockerfile.v6
       arg) or by cloud-init from the pinned commit it checked out. This is
       the production path; Codex diff iter-2 P1 noted that the container
       does NOT have .git because Dockerfile.v6 only COPYs src/scripts/config.
    2. .git/HEAD probe in /opt/polaris or cwd — local-dev path.
    3. "unknown" — fail-soft.
    """
    explicit = os.environ.get("POLARIS_GIT_COMMIT", "").strip()
    if explicit:
        return explicit[:12]
    for candidate in [Path("/opt/polaris"), Path.cwd()]:
        head_file = candidate / ".git" / "HEAD"
        if head_file.exists():
            head = head_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8").strip()
            if head.startswith("ref: "):
                ref_path = candidate / ".git" / head[5:]
                if ref_path.exists():
                    return ref_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").strip()[:12]
            return head[:12]
    return "unknown"


def _signing_key_info() -> tuple[str | None, str | None]:
    """Read POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID and look up the fingerprint via gpg if available."""
    key_id = os.environ.get("POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID", "").strip() or None
    if not key_id:
        return None, None
    # The env var IS the fingerprint per I-carney-005 convention.
    return key_id, key_id


def _sovereignty_policy() -> SovereigntyPolicy:
    """Encodes the artifact_to_slice_chain filter constants for reviewers."""
    return SovereigntyPolicy(
        cleared_tiers=["T1"],
        cascade_rule=(
            "Sentences citing non-T1 sources are redacted from the bundle. "
            "Sections with zero passing sentences are dropped. Reports "
            "with zero passing sections raise "
            "SovereigntyFilterEmptiedReportError (HTTP 422)."
        ),
        tier_definitions={
            "T1": "Regulatory + clinical guideline corpora (FDA, EMA, NICE, etc.) — legal-cleared by default",
            "T2": "Peer-reviewed primary literature — requires explicit operator clearance",
            "T3": "Unverified web content + raw uploads — defaults excluded; falls back to T3 on unknown tiers",
        },
    )


def _read_pubkey() -> str:
    """Read the armored GPG public key, falling back to gpg --export.

    Per Codex iter-2 P1-002: cloud-init writes the file alongside the GPG
    import, but local dev may not have the file. Fall back to shelling out
    to gpg if POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID is set. Strict 503 if neither works.
    """
    path = Path(os.environ.get("POLARIS_GPG_PUBKEY_PATH", DEFAULT_PUBKEY_PATH))
    if path.exists():
        return path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    key_id = os.environ.get("POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID", "").strip()
    if key_id:
        try:
            result = subprocess.run(
                ["gpg", "--armor", "--export", key_id],
                capture_output=True,
                text=True,
                timeout=10,
                check=False,
            )
            if result.returncode == 0 and "BEGIN PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK" in result.stdout:
                return result.stdout
        except Exception:
            pass
    raise HTTPException(
        status_code=503,
        detail={
            "error": "pubkey_unavailable",
            "message": (
                f"Public key not found at {path} and gpg --export fallback failed. "
                "Run scripts/bootstrap_gpg_demo_key.sh on the operator workstation "
                "and confirm cloud-init imported the key."
            ),
        },
    )


@router.get("", response_model=TransparencyResponse)
def transparency() -> TransparencyResponse:
    """Public deploy descriptor."""
    key_id, fpr = _signing_key_info()
    # I-carney-008 Codex iter-1 P1-2: treat empty-string env vars as missing
    # (otherwise an unfilled .env template surfaces provider="" / region="").
    provider = os.environ.get("POLARIS_PROVIDER", "").strip() or "unknown"
    region = (
        os.environ.get("POLARIS_REGION", "").strip()
        or os.environ.get("AWS_REGION", "").strip()
        or "unknown"
    )
    # I-carney-008 Codex iter-2 P2-2: surface whether the build-time block
    # (GitHub, Docker registry, Cloudflare CDN, pypi/npm/debian) was pruned
    # from the runtime allowlist by scripts/egress_runtime_tighten.sh.
    pruned_flag = Path(
        os.environ.get(
            "POLARIS_RUNTIME_PRUNED_FLAG", "/etc/polaris/runtime_pruned.flag"
        )
    )
    return TransparencyResponse(
        provider=provider,
        region=region,
        git_commit=_git_commit(),
        polaris_version=__version__,
        deploy_timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        signing_key_id=key_id,
        signing_key_fingerprint=fpr,
        sovereignty_filter=_sovereignty_policy(),
        evaluator_models={
            "generator": os.environ.get("PG_GENERATOR_MODEL", "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro"),
            "evaluator": os.environ.get("PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL", "qwen/qwen-2.5-72b-instruct"),
        },
        egress_allowlist=_load_egress_allowlist(),
        build_time_hosts_pruned=pruned_flag.exists(),
        dependencies=_load_dependencies(),
    )


@router.get("/pubkey.asc", response_class=PlainTextResponse)
def pubkey() -> PlainTextResponse:
    """Armored GPG public key for bundle signature verification."""
    body = _read_pubkey()
    return PlainTextResponse(content=body, media_type="text/plain; charset=us-ascii")


class PolicyResponse(BaseModel):
    """Full sovereignty + egress policy with version string."""

    version: str
    sovereignty_filter: SovereigntyPolicy
    egress_allowlist: list[str]
    enforcement_layer: list[str]


@router.get("/policy", response_model=PolicyResponse)
def policy() -> PolicyResponse:
    return PolicyResponse(
        version=POLICY_VERSION,
        sovereignty_filter=_sovereignty_policy(),
        egress_allowlist=_load_egress_allowlist(),
        enforcement_layer=[
            "host iptables OUTPUT chain (scripts/egress_lockdown.sh)",
            "Docker DOCKER-USER chain (scripts/egress_lockdown.sh)",
        ],
    )

 succeeded in 1390ms:

def test_transparency_egress_allowlist_uses_env_override(
    app_with_transparency, tmp_path, monkeypatch
):
    allowlist_path = tmp_path / "egress_allowlist.txt"
    allowlist_path.write_text(
        "# comment\n"
        "openrouter.ai\n"
        "google.serper.dev\n"
        "\n"
        "api.semanticscholar.org\n",
        encoding="utf-8",
    )
    monkeypatch.setenv("POLARIS_EGRESS_ALLOWLIST", str(allowlist_path))
    client = TestClient(app_with_transparency)
    resp = client.get("/transparency")
    body = resp.json()
    assert "openrouter.ai" in body["egress_allowlist"]
    assert "google.serper.dev" in body["egress_allowlist"]
    assert "api.semanticscholar.org" in body["egress_allowlist"]
    assert len(body["egress_allowlist"]) == 3  # comment + blank skipped


def test_transparency_egress_allowlist_default_path_is_in_container(
    app_with_transparency, monkeypatch
):
    """Codex diff iter-1 P1-004 production-default test: with no env override
    and no /app/config/egress_allowlist.txt file (test env doesn't have it),
    the response must NOT be 'unrestricted' if the path is reachable. We
    verify the DEFAULT path is the in-container path (/app/config/...),
    not /etc/polaris/* which the container cannot see.
    """
    from polaris_v6.api import transparency as tmod
    assert tmod.DEFAULT_EGRESS_ALLOWLIST.startswith("/app/"), (

# I-carney-005 — POLARIS v6 backend image (api + worker share this image).
#
# Single image, two entrypoint commands:
#   api    → uvicorn polaris_v6.api.app:app
#   worker → dramatiq polaris_v6.queue.actors
#
# Pipeline B legacy image (Dockerfile + docker_entrypoint.sh + docker-compose.yml)
# stays untouched per I-carney-005 acceptance criterion 8.

FROM python:3.11-slim

# System deps:
#   gnupg          — GPG signing of audit bundles
#   curl           — healthcheck + diagnostic
#   build-essential — wheel builds for pipeline-A native deps
#   libpango/cairo + libffi/gdk-pixbuf — WeasyPrint (pipeline-A PDF export)
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    gnupg \
    curl \
    build-essential \
    libpango-1.0-0 \
    libpangocairo-1.0-0 \
    libgdk-pixbuf2.0-0 \
    libffi-dev \
    libcairo2 \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

# Install BOTH requirement files (P1-002 from Codex iter-2 brief):
#   requirements.txt    — pipeline-A surface (langchain, numpy, openai,
#                          aiohttp, python-gnupg, ...)
#   requirements-v6.txt — v6 spine (fastapi, dramatiq, redis-py, OTLP, ...)
#
# Codex diff iter-1 P1-A: requirements.txt pins protobuf<5.0.0 because of
# google-generativeai's transitive constraint; requirements-v6.txt pins
# protobuf==6.33.6 for OTLP semconv. Conflict resolution: strip google-
# generativeai (pipeline-C dead code per src/orchestration/FROZEN; only
# src/llm/gemini_client.py + src/utils/atomic_decomposer.py import it,
# neither reachable from pipeline-A or v6 backend) AND its protobuf<5
# constraint from the v6 image only. Pipeline-B's separate `Dockerfile`
# is untouched and retains both deps.
COPY requirements.txt requirements-v6.txt ./
RUN sed -e '/^google-generativeai/d' \
        -e '/^protobuf</d' \
        requirements.txt > /tmp/requirements-v6-pipeline-a.txt && \
    pip install --no-cache-dir -r /tmp/requirements-v6-pipeline-a.txt -r requirements-v6.txt

# Copy application code.
COPY src/ src/
COPY scripts/ scripts/
COPY config/ config/
COPY architecture.md ground_rules.md ./

# PYTHONPATH so `from polaris_v6...` and `from polaris_graph...` resolve
# without --app-dir flag (P2 from Codex iter-1 approved).
ENV PYTHONPATH=/app/src:/app

# Default broker URL points to the compose service `redis`. Override at
# `docker run` / compose time with POLARIS_V6_REDIS_URL.
ENV POLARIS_V6_REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379/0

# Dedicated writable GPG homedir (P1-006 from Codex iter-1).
# `docker-compose.v6.yml` bind-mounts the operator's host keyring here.
ENV GNUPGHOME=/app/gpg
RUN mkdir -p /app/gpg && chmod 700 /app/gpg

RUN mkdir -p /app/outputs /app/logs /app/state /app/data

EXPOSE 8000

HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=20s --retries=3 \
    CMD curl -fsS http://localhost:8000/health || exit 1

COPY scripts/v6_entrypoint.sh /entrypoint.sh
RUN chmod +x /entrypoint.sh
ENTRYPOINT ["/entrypoint.sh"]
CMD ["api"]

 succeeded in 879ms:
# I-carney-005 — POLARIS v6 production stack (Carney demo).
#
# Stack: redis + api + worker + webui.
# Legacy pipeline-B compose (docker-compose.yml) stays untouched per
# I-carney-005 acceptance criterion 8.
#
# Bring up:  docker compose -f docker-compose.v6.yml up -d
# Tear down: docker compose -f docker-compose.v6.yml down -v   (NB: drops volumes)

services:
  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    command:
      - redis-server
      - --appendonly
      - "yes"
      - --save
      - "60"
      - "1000"
    volumes:
      - redis_data:/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 10
    restart: unless-stopped

  api:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile.v6
    command: api
    env_file:
      - .env
    environment:
      POLARIS_V6_REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379/0
      POLARIS_V6_RUN_DB: /app/state/v6_runs.sqlite
      GNUPGHOME: /app/gpg
      # POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID is read from .env via env_file
    volumes:
      - shared_state:/app/state
      - ./outputs:/app/outputs
      - ./logs:/app/logs
      - ./data:/app/data
      # P1-006: writable GPG homedir bind-mount.
      - ${POLARIS_GPG_HOMEDIR:-~/.gnupg-polaris}:/app/gpg
      # I-carney-004 P1: mount /etc/polaris/static_accounts.yaml into the
      # container so verify_app_startup() can find the auth substrate.
      # Read-only bind; host file is 640 root:root, container reads as root.
      - ${POLARIS_ETC_DIR:-/etc/polaris}:/etc/polaris:ro
    ports:
      - "${POLARIS_API_PORT:-8000}:8000"
    depends_on:
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-fsS", "http://localhost:8000/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      start_period: 30s
      retries: 3
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 6G
        reservations:
          memory: 2G

  worker:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile.v6
    command: worker
    env_file:
      - .env
    environment:
      POLARIS_V6_REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379/0
      POLARIS_V6_RUN_DB: /app/state/v6_runs.sqlite
      GNUPGHOME: /app/gpg
    volumes:
      - shared_state:/app/state
      - ./outputs:/app/outputs
      - ./logs:/app/logs
      - ./data:/app/data
      - ${POLARIS_GPG_HOMEDIR:-~/.gnupg-polaris}:/app/gpg
      - ${POLARIS_ETC_DIR:-/etc/polaris}:/etc/polaris:ro
    depends_on:
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
      api:
        condition: service_healthy
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 8G
        reservations:
          memory: 4G

  webui:
    build:
      context: ./web
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
      args:
        # P1-B from Codex diff iter-2: Next.js bakes rewrites() destinations
        # at build time, so INTERNAL_API_URL must be a build arg. NOT
